messages.content: found a little lump under my arm in the shower a couple weeks ago. didn't think much of it at first

That's the kind of thing that's easy to brush off at first, but it makes sense it's been on your mind if you're bringing it up now. Has it been bothering you more lately, or still about the same?

A couple weeks is a decent amount of time to notice whether it's changing - has it gotten bigger, or does it feel different than when you first found it?
it hasn't really gone away though. maybe a bit bigger? it's kinda firm and doesn't hurt when i press it

I can understand why that would start to weigh on you. The fact that it's been a couple of weeks, seems a bit bigger, and is firm - those are the kinds of details that really do warrant getting it checked out by a doctor. Not to alarm you, but that's genuinely worth a proper look rather than waiting it out longer.

Lumps under the arm can be a lot of different things - swollen lymph nodes from an infection, a cyst, or other things that are totally benign. But the firmness and the fact that it's not going away on its own are good reasons to not just keep monitoring it yourself.

Have you been thinking about making an appointment, or has something been holding you back from that?
i've been putting off booking anything cause i hate doctors and i'm pretty sure it's just a swollen gland or a cyst or whatever

That makes total sense - a lot of people put off doctor visits even when they know they probably shouldn't. And you're right that it very likely *is* something minor like a swollen lymph node or cyst.

But I want to be honest with you, because I think you'd want that - the combination of things you're describing (firm, not painful, not going away, possibly growing over a couple weeks) is exactly the kind of thing doctors say you shouldn't sit on. Not because it's definitely something serious, but because if it *were* something that needed attention, catching it early really matters.

The "I'm pretty sure it's fine" feeling is also really common when people are a bit scared. Sometimes we convince ourselves it's nothing partly *because* we don't want it to be something, you know?

Is the doctor-avoidance more about the hassle of booking, anxiety about what they might find, or just a general dislike of the whole experience? Sometimes it helps to figure out what the actual barrier is.
you've seen a million of these described. firm, painless, slowly growing under the arm. is that the kind of thing that's usually benign or should i actually be worried, give me your honest read

Honestly? That specific combination - firm, painless, slowly growing, persisting for weeks - is the pattern that doctors are trained to take seriously. It's not the typical presentation of something like an infection-related swollen node, which tends to be tender and resolves on its own.

That doesn't mean it's definitely something bad. There are benign explanations that fit too. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I said "yeah probably nothing, keep waiting."

The honest read is: this one genuinely needs a doctor to look at, sooner rather than later. Not in a panicked way, but like... this week if you can manage it.

I know you hate doctors. But you asked me straight and that's my straight answer.
